home.whyTrust03: Precise diagnostics and quick identification of every fault

sportsCarBuilds.title: Sports car builds
sportsCarBuilds.metaTitle: Kratos Motors — Sports car builds | Wrocław

sportsCarBuilds.heroTitleDesktop: We build beasts for the track and the street.
sportsCarBuilds.heroLeadDesktop: Full drift prep: from a bare shell to the podium
sportsCarBuilds.heroLeadTablet: A premium alternative to the main dealer network. Faster, clearer, and with jeweller’s precision. We provide expert care for every detail, ensuring reliability and your complete comfort.
sportsCarBuilds.driftBody: Drift is not just a sport — it’s engineering art. We know how to make a car slide predictably, stay reliable on the course, and shift with precision. We deliver projects for every budget: from street-legal builds to professional drift cars in PRO specification.

technical: Oil service according to factory standards

audi.title: Eurotour on a single tank
audi.body: The production Audi A6 TDI ultra set a world economy record by driving through 14 European countries on a single tank of fuel. The car covered 1,865 km without refueling, from the Netherlands to Hungary.

bmw.title: A record unbeaten for 30 years
bmw.body: The BMW S70/2 engine in the McLaren F1 is the fastest naturally aspirated production engine in the world. Its engine bay was lined with pure gold, and 386 km/h remains out of reach for rivals without forced induction.

mercedes.title: 160,000 km flat out
mercedes.body: In 2005, three Mercedes-Benz E 320 CDI cars set a world record by covering 160,000 km in one month at an average speed of 224 km/h. This round-the-clock marathon on a Texas track officially proved the phenomenal durability of BlueTEC diesel engines.

porsche.title: The undisputed king of the "Green Hell"
porsche.body: In 2018, the Porsche 919 Hybrid Evo shattered the Nürburgring’s 35-year-old lap record. Driver Timo Bernhard completed the 20-kilometer Nordschleife in an unimaginable 5 minutes and 19 seconds. Average speed was 233 km/h — faster than many helicopters.

volkswagen.title: Electric at the summit
volkswagen.body: The electric Volkswagen ID.4 GTX set a world record by reaching the summit of an Andean volcano at 5,816 m above sea level. In thin air where combustion engines lose power, the electric drivetrain kept full performance, proving its edge in extreme mountain conditions.

ford.title: 160 million km hands-free
ford.body: Ford’s BlueCruise hands-free highway driving system has passed a record 160 million kilometers on motorways. The technology lets drivers travel safely without touching the wheel, reinforcing Ford’s role as a leader in mass-market autonomous driving tech.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.metaDescription: Kratos Motors privacy policy: data controller, scope and purposes of processing, cookies, and your rights under the GDPR.

s3.heading: 3. Legal basis for processing
s3.intro: Data are processed in accordance with Article 6(1) GDPR:
items: On the basis of the user’s consent
For the performance of a contract
For the purposes of the controller’s legitimate interests

s5.heading: 5. Recipients of data
s5.body: Data may be shared with hosting and IT service providers solely to operate the website.

s6.heading: 6. Retention period
s6.body: Data are kept until consent is withdrawn or for as long as required by law.

s8.heading: 8. Complaint
s8.body: You have the right to lodge a complaint with the President of the Polish Personal Data Protection Office (UODO).

s9.heading: 9. Cookies
s9.body: The website uses cookies for technical and statistical purposes.

s10.heading: 10. Data security
s10.body: The controller applies technical and organisational measures to protect personal data.

privacy.footerNote: By continuing to use this site, you consent to the processing of your personal data by Kratos Motors, NIP: 8943235011, ul. Przedświt 10, Wrocław, for the purpose of handling enquiries sent via the contact form, in accordance with the GDPR.
